name: Low Level (Low Level Learning)
oneLine: Cybersecurity and reverse-engineering educator publishing approachable deep-dives on memory, exploits, and systems internals.
bio: Ed runs the channel formerly known as Low Level Learning, now branded Low Level (@LowLevelTV), where he covers reverse engineering, binary exploitation, C, memory layout, and CVE breakdowns. He works as a software security engineer and presents content that bridges entry-level explainers (pointers, syscalls) and serious offensive-security material (Ghidra, IDA, fuzzing). The channel has grown past 1M subscribers across YouTube and Twitch.
style: Edited talking-head and screen-recorded walkthroughs, often using Ghidra/IDA, with whiteboard-style diagrams for memory and system concepts.
